title: File-Naming Considerations on Unix
breadcrumbs: File and Directory Naming Considerations on Specific Platforms
text: File-Naming Considerations on Unix Unix uses the following naming conventions: File: { Directory / } FileName , for example uniface/readme . Directory: Directory / . In path and file names, periods and other special characters are passed 'as is' to the operating system. Uniface does not check the syntax of any such path name or file name. To keep file and directory names as generic as possible in file management ProcScript, use Uniface logicals in your assignment file to prefix the names with a Unix-specific home. See Guidelines for Writing Platform-Independent ProcScript . Related Links Syntax of File and Directory Names You can choose to specify file and directory names generically or using platform-specific names—the ProcScript statements and functions for local file system management accept both.
